trivia_bullets.text: Scrap Compactor is a colorless artifact ({1} mana cost) set on Avishkar (formerly Kaladesh), the technological home-plane of the Ghirapur Grand Prix in Aetherdrift. Avishkar's aesthetic is defined by Kaladesh's signature gold filigree design language, visible in the card art's ornate gold machine body. [Scryfall; Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 1]

Flavor text (Scryfall): "From the screaming of Duskmourn's possessed metal came the happy cries of freed souls." This is a direct cross-plane continuity reference: Duskmourn: House of Horror (2024) established that the sentient horror-house plane possessed metal objects and trapped souls within them. The Scrap Compactor's function — breaking down metal to release what's inside — maps onto that lore beat, implying Avishkar's scrappers processed haunted metal salvaged after the Phyrexian and Duskmourn events. [Scryfall]

Scrap Compactor offers two sacrifice modes: {3},{T} deals 3 damage to target creature; {6},{T} destroys target creature or Vehicle. The second mode is notable for explicitly targeting Vehicles, which is relevant in Aetherdrift's Vehicle-heavy environment (41 Vehicles in the set — more than had been printed across all previous Magic expansions combined before Aetherdrift). [Scryfall oracle_text; Draftsim Aetherdrift set review]

Artist Viko Menezes rendered the card in warm gold-and-blue Kaladesh palette, with domed city architecture in the background consistent with Ghirapur's skyline as established in Kaladesh (2016) and Aether Revolt (2017). The cross-plane flavor text — written from an Aetherdrift perspective about Duskmourn's past events — is an example of Aetherdrift's design strategy of treating the multiverse as a continuous narrative space across recent sets. [Scryfall artist field; Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 2]
